metadata.name: mind_voyager_hybrid_edge_weights
metadata.description: Reference manifest for edge weights used in the hybrid topic graph. These weights are heuristic design choices from scripts/build_hybrid_topic_graph.py, not learned parameters.

ontology.description: Weights inherited unchanged from the original ontology graph builder.

contains.justification: Taxonomic membership between a super-topic and its sub-topics is treated as exact structural containment.

associated_with.justification: Used for ontology-level topical association. 0.9 marks stronger subtopic-to-belief association; 0.8 marks slightly weaker subtopic-to-intermediate or coping association.

evokes.justification: Subtopics evoke emotional themes, but that relation is treated as plausible rather than deterministic.

manifests_as.justification: Subtopics can manifest as behavior patterns, but the mapping is thematic rather than one-to-one.

suggests_prompt_type.justification: Prompt-type links are recommendation strength signals rather than case-structural facts. 0.7 is used for direct subtopic recommendations; 0.65 for broader concept-to-prompt links.

leads_to.justification: Belief cluster to intermediate-belief-type links express likely conceptual progression, but not strict determinism.

maintained_by.justification: Intermediate belief types are maintained by coping patterns with moderate conceptual strength.

dataset_grounded.description: Explicit structural edges tying dataset rows to case, patient, and statement nodes.

has_case_record.justification: A patient-profile node explicitly owns each case record.

has_external_episode.justification: Each case record explicitly contains one external episode node.

has_history.justification: History text is copied directly from the dataset row.

has_core_belief.justification: Core belief statements are explicitly listed in the dataset row.

has_intermediate_belief_default.justification: The default intermediate belief field is explicit dataset content.

has_intermediate_belief_depression.justification: The depression-specific intermediate belief field is explicit dataset content.

has_coping_strategy.justification: Coping strategy text is copied directly from the dataset row.

has_situation.justification: Situation text is explicit external episode content from the dataset row.

has_automatic_thought.justification: Automatic thought text is explicit external episode content from the dataset row.

has_emotion.justification: Emotion statements are explicit external episode content from the dataset row.

has_behavior.justification: Behavior text is explicit external episode content from the dataset row.

bridge.description: Heuristic edges connecting dataset-grounded statements to ontology nodes.

instance_of.justification: These mappings are inferred normalization links, not explicit annotations. Core belief mappings are given the highest weight because the belief-to-cluster mapping table is explicit and small. Emotion and intermediate-belief mappings are moderately strong lexical/semantic matches. Coping and behavior mappings are weaker because they are derived from broader heuristic phrase matching.

associated_with_topic.justification: These are soft topical alignment links. Situation-to-topic is strongest because situations often contain explicit concrete domain cues. Automatic-thought links are somewhat weaker because they are interpretive. History links are weakest because history text is broader and may span multiple themes.

causal.description: Case-structure edges inferred from the semantics of CBT case formulation fields.

history_supports_belief.justification: Relevant history is treated as a strong but not deterministic developmental support for core beliefs.

belief_expressed_as.justification: Intermediate beliefs are modeled as reasonably strong expressions of core beliefs, but the relation is still interpretive.

intermediate_shapes_thought.justification: Intermediate beliefs are taken to strongly shape automatic thoughts in a given episode.

triggers_automatic_thought.justification: Within the row-level CBT episode structure, the situation is treated as the direct trigger for the recorded automatic thought.

evokes_emotion.justification: Automatic thoughts are treated as very strong elicitors of the recorded emotions, though not strictly one-to-one.

influences_behavior.justification: Automatic thoughts strongly influence behavior, but behavior may also reflect coping style and broader context.

manifests_as_behavior.justification: Coping strategy is treated as a strong contributor to behavior, but not the sole determinant.
